qa_pairs.question: Where can I find information about the differences between StdEnv/2023 and earlier standard environments?
qa_pairs.answer: The differences between StdEnv/2023 and earlier environments are discussed in [[Standard software environments]].

qa_pairs.question: When will StdEnv/2023 automatically become the default environment for all clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: After April 1, 2024, StdEnv/2023 will be the default environment for all clusters.

qa_pairs.question: How can I set my own default standard environment?
qa_pairs.answer: You can specify your own default environment at any time by modifying the $HOME/.modulerc file.

qa_pairs.question: What command can I use to set my default environment to StdEnv/2020?
qa_pairs.answer: You can set your default environment to StdEnv/2020 by running the command: echo "module-version StdEnv/2020 default" >> $HOME/.modulerc.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are required for a change to the default standard environment in $HOME/.modulerc to take effect?
qa_pairs.answer: You must log out and log in again for changes to your default standard environment in $HOME/.modulerc to take effect.

qa_pairs.question: Do I need to recompile my code when the standard environment (StdEnv) changes?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you compile your own code, you should recompile it with the newest version of the standard environment when the StdEnv changes.

qa_pairs.question: What action should I take regarding R or Python packages when the StdEnv changes?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have installed R or Python packages, you should reinstall them with the newest version of the standard environment when the StdEnv changes.

qa_pairs.question: How can I continue to use an earlier standard environment for an existing workflow?
qa_pairs.answer: To use an earlier environment for an existing workflow, simply add 'module load StdEnv/2020' to your job scripts before loading any other modules.

qa_pairs.question: Will older standard environments be removed?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the earlier environments and any software dependent on them will remain available.

qa_pairs.question: Are all earlier standard environment versions still supported?
qa_pairs.answer: No, versions 2016.4 and 2018.3 of the standard environments are no longer supported, and it is recommended not to use them.

qa_pairs.question: In which environment will staff install new software?
qa_pairs.answer: Our staff will only install software in the new environment 2023.

qa_pairs.question: Can I mix modules from different standard environments within a single job?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you should use a single environment for a given job. Mixing modules from different environments is not recommended.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I try to mix modules from different standard environments?
qa_pairs.answer: The results of trying to mix different environments are unpredictable but will generally lead to errors.

qa_pairs.question: Can different jobs use different standard environments?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, different jobs can use different standard environments by explicitly loading one at the job's beginning.

qa_pairs.question: Which standard environment should I use if I am starting a new project?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are starting a new project, you should use StdEnv/2023.

qa_pairs.question: How do I specify StdEnv/2023 in my job scripts?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use StdEnv/2023 by adding 'module load StdEnv/2023' to your job scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Is it necessary to remove 'module load StdEnv/2023' from job scripts after April 1, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the 'module load StdEnv/2023' command does not need to be deleted to use StdEnv/2023 after April 1.

qa_pairs.question: Is it recommended to load modules in my .bashrc file to maintain an older environment?
qa_pairs.answer: No, loading modules in your .bashrc is not recommended. Instead, explicitly load modules in your job scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Does this standard environment change affect users who only use cloud resources and not HPC clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, this change will only affect the [[Available software]] accessed by [[Using modules| using environment modules]] on HPC clusters, not users who use cloud resources only.

qa_pairs.question: Why might I no longer be able to load a module I previously used?
qa_pairs.answer: More recent versions of most applications are installed in the new environment, which might cause previous modules to be unavailable or different.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check the available versions of modules, such as GCC?
qa_pairs.answer: To see the available versions of modules, you can run the 'module avail' command. For example, 'module avail gcc' will show several versions of GCC compilers.
